q: I threw 7 no-hitters in my MLB career, more than any pitcher in history. Who am I?
This player holds the MLB record with 4,256 career hits. Name him.
Called 'The Sultan of Swat', this Yankees legend changed baseball forever. Name him.
I played 2,632 consecutive games, breaking Lou Gehrig's record. Who am I?
He hit 73 home runs in 2001, setting the single-season MLB record. Who was he?
Called 'The Kid', this Seattle Mariners outfielder was known for his sweet left-handed swing. Name him.
This player holds the all-time MLB record with 762 career home runs. Name him.
I hit a walk-off home run in the bottom of the 9th to win Game 7 of the 1960 World Series for the Pirates. Who am I?
Called 'The Iron Horse', this Yankees first baseman played 2,130 consecutive games. Name him.
This Dodgers pitcher threw a perfect game in 1965 and won three Cy Young Awards. Name him.
I broke the color barrier in Major League Baseball when I debuted for the Brooklyn Dodgers on April 15, 1947. Who am I?
This pitcher holds the all-time MLB record with 5,714 career strikeouts. Name him.
Called 'The Say Hey Kid', this Giants center fielder made one of baseball's most famous catches in the 1954 World Series. Name him.
This manager led the Chicago Cubs to their first World Series title in 108 years in 2016. Who was he?
I played 20 seasons for the New York Yankees and finished with 3,465 career hits. Who am I?
This Dodgers outfielder hit a pinch-hit walk-off home run in Game 1 of the 1988 World Series despite barely being able to walk. Name him.
He holds the MLB record with 56 consecutive games with a hit, set in 1941. Who was he?
Called 'Hammerin' Hank', this outfielder held the career home run record with 755 for over 30 years. Name him.
This pitcher holds the record for most career wins in MLB history with 511. Name him.
I was the first player in MLB history to hit 60 home runs in a season, doing so in 1927. Who am I?
This Red Sox outfielder batted .406 in 1941, the last player to hit over .400 in a season. Name him.
Called 'The Big Unit', this 6-foot-10 left-hander struck out 4,875 batters in his career. Name him.
This Red Sox designated hitter hit a walk-off home run in the 12th inning of Game 4 of the 2004 ALCS against the Yankees. Name him.
I stole 1,406 bases in my career, more than any other player in MLB history. Who am I?
He recorded 652 career saves, the most in MLB history at the time of his retirement. Who was he?
Called 'Mr. October', this outfielder hit three home runs on three consecutive pitches in Game 6 of the 1977 World Series. Name him.
This Pittsburgh Pirates outfielder died in a plane crash on New Year's Eve 1972 while delivering aid to earthquake victims in Nicaragua. Name him.
I threw a perfect game in the 1956 World Series, the only one in postseason history. Who am I?
This manager holds the record for most career wins in MLB history with 3,731. Name him.
Called 'Charlie Hustle', this switch-hitter holds the record for most career hits in MLB history. Name him.
This Braves pitcher won four consecutive Cy Young Awards from 1992 to 1995. Name him.
This Cardinals first baseman set the record with 70 home runs in 1998 before Barry Bonds broke it. Name him.
I won the AL Triple Crown in 1967, leading the league in batting average, home runs, and RBIs for the Red Sox. Who am I?
This Negro Leagues legend was known as the fastest pitcher of his era and finally debuted in the MLB at age 42. Name him.
Called 'The Rocket', this pitcher won seven Cy Young Awards during his career. Name him.
This Cubs shortstop hit 512 career home runs and was famous for saying 'Let's play two!' Name him.
This Angels outfielder has won three AL MVP Awards and is considered one of the greatest players of his generation. Name him.
This Negro Leagues catcher was called 'the Black Babe Ruth' and reportedly hit nearly 800 home runs in his career. Name him.
He holds the record for most Gold Glove Awards by a pitcher with 18. Who is he?
Called 'Big Papi', this designated hitter was the 2013 World Series MVP for the Red Sox. Name him.
I won the NL MVP Award seven times, more than any other player in history. Who am I?
This pitcher threw a perfect game on May 18, 2004, for the Arizona Diamondbacks at age 40. Name him.
He managed the Yankees to seven World Series titles between 1949 and 1960. Who was he?
This Cardinals pitcher had a 1.12 ERA in 1968, the lowest in the live-ball era. Name him.
I collected 262 hits in 2004, breaking George Sisler's single-season record. Who am I?
This third baseman won 16 consecutive Gold Glove Awards from 1960 to 1975. Name him.
Called 'The Wizard of Oz', this Cardinals shortstop was famous for his acrobatic defense. Name him.
This Padres outfielder won eight NL batting titles and finished with a career .338 batting average. Name him.
I pitched the 'Bloody Sock' game in the 2004 ALCS, defeating the Yankees in Game 6 with a sutured ankle tendon. Who am I?
This catcher holds the record for most games caught in MLB history with 2,427. Name him.
Called 'The Georgia Peach', this outfielder retired with a .366 career batting average, the highest in MLB history. Name him.
He pitched 59.1 consecutive scoreless innings in the regular season in 1988, breaking Don Drysdale's record. Who was he?

This outfielder holds the record for most career walks in MLB history with 2,558. Name him.
He threw a no-hitter at age 44, making him the oldest pitcher to accomplish the feat in MLB history. Who was he?
This Phillies third baseman won back-to-back NL MVP Awards in 1980 and 1981. Name him.
I hit a game-winning single in the bottom of the 9th in Game 7 of the 2001 World Series against Mariano Rivera. Who am I?
Called 'The Big Hurt', this first baseman won back-to-back AL MVP Awards in 1993 and 1994 for the White Sox. Name him.
He hit 660 career home runs and won two NL MVP Awards while playing for the Giants. Who was he?
This Cardinals pitcher struck out 17 batters in Game 1 of the 1968 World Series. Name him.
This pitcher was the first player unanimously elected to the Baseball Hall of Fame, receiving all 425 votes in 2019. Name him.
Called 'Yogi', this Yankees catcher won three AL MVP Awards and appeared in 14 World Series. Name him.
He won the AL Cy Young Award in 1999 and 2000 while pitching for the Red Sox, with ERAs of 2.07 and 1.74. Who was he?

Called 'Shoeless Joe', this outfielder was banned from baseball for his alleged role in the 1919 Black Sox scandal. Name him.
This manager led the Yankees to four World Series titles in five years from 1996 to 2000. Name him.
He stole 130 bases in 1982, setting the single-season MLB record. Who was he?
Called 'Stan the Man', this Cardinals legend finished his career with 3,630 hits and a .331 batting average. Name him.
This player hit 61 home runs in 1961, breaking Babe Ruth's single-season record. Name him.
I struck out 20 batters in a nine-inning game twice in my career, first doing it in 1986 for the Red Sox. Who am I?
This Tigers outfielder won the AL Triple Crown in 2012, the first since Yastrzemski in 1967. Name him.
This left-handed pitcher won 363 career games, the most by any left-hander in MLB history. Name him.
Called 'Mr. Cub', this shortstop never played in a postseason game despite a Hall of Fame career. Name him.
This player hit the 'Shot Heard Round the World' home run to win the 1951 NL pennant for the New York Giants. Name him.
He holds the record for most career doubles in MLB history with 792. Who is he?
This pitcher holds the record for most career shutouts in MLB history with 110. Name him.
Called 'A-Rod', this shortstop and third baseman hit 696 career home runs. Name him.
I won the NL Cy Young Award three times and held the lowest career ERA among active starters for many years. Who am I?

Called 'Pee Wee', this Dodgers shortstop famously put his arm around Jackie Robinson in a show of support. Name him.
I hold the record for most career grand slams in MLB history with 23. Who am I?

Called 'Tom Terrific', this Mets pitcher won three NL Cy Young Awards and led the Miracle Mets to the 1969 World Series. Name him.

Called 'The Captain', this Yankees shortstop made his MLB debut in 1995 and retired after the 2014 season. Name him.
I hit 714 career home runs and held the all-time record for 39 years until Hank Aaron surpassed me. Who am I?

This first baseman won three NL MVP Awards and hit 475 home runs during his time with the Cardinals. Name him.

This switch-hitting outfielder won the Triple Crown in 1956 with a .353 average, 52 home runs, and 130 RBIs. Name him.
Called 'The Pride of the Yankees', this first baseman's farewell speech included the line 'I consider myself the luckiest man on the face of the earth.' Name him.
I hold the record for most career RBIs in MLB history with 2,297. Who am I?
This Royals third baseman hit .390 in 1980, the highest batting average since Ted Williams hit .406 in 1941. Name him.
Called 'The Mick', this switch-hitter hit 536 career home runs and won three AL MVP Awards for the Yankees. Name him.
I won the NL Cy Young Award four times while pitching for the Phillies. Who am I?
This Negro Leagues outfielder is considered by many historians to be the greatest all-around player in black baseball history. He played from 1915 to 1941. Name him.
He won the NL Rookie of the Year in 1947, the first year the award was given. Who was he?

This pitcher won the NL Cy Young Award in 2014, 2015, and 2017 while playing for the Nationals. Name him.
He was the first African American player in the American League, debuting for the Cleveland Indians on July 5, 1947. Who was he?

Called 'Pudge', this catcher won 13 Gold Glove Awards and the 1999 AL MVP. Name him.
I threw a perfect game on May 29, 2010, and also threw a no-hitter in the 2010 NLDS. Who am I?

This pitcher holds the record for most career complete games in MLB history with 749. Name him.
I hit a walk-off home run in the bottom of the 9th in Game 6 of the 1993 World Series for the Blue Jays. Who am I?

Called 'Teddy Ballgame', this outfielder homered in his final career at-bat on September 28, 1960. Name him.

This outfielder won both the NL and AL MVP Awards, the first player to win the MVP in both leagues. Name him.

Called 'The Freak', this Giants pitcher won back-to-back NL Cy Young Awards in 2008 and 2009. Name him.

He holds the career record for most intentional walks received with 688. Who is he?

I hold the record for most career runs scored in MLB history with 2,295. Who am I?

He batted .350 with 242 hits in his rookie season for the Mariners in 2001, winning both AL MVP and Rookie of the Year. Who was he?
This third baseman hit a dramatic walk-off home run in the 11th inning of Game 6 of the 2011 World Series for the Cardinals. Name him.

I hold the single-season record for most walks drawn with 232, set in 2004. Who am I?

Called 'Catfish', this pitcher threw a perfect game in 1968 and won three World Series with the Oakland A's. Name him.

This outfielder won 12 AL batting titles during his career, the most in American League history. Name him.

This first baseman hit 612 career home runs, the eighth-most in MLB history, playing for the Indians, Phillies, and White Sox among others. Name him.

This A's pitcher won both the AL Cy Young and MVP in 1971 at age 21. Name him.

I am the only pitcher to throw no-hitters in three consecutive seasons (1973, 1974, 1975) while pitching for the Angels. Who am I?

This outfielder set the NL single-season stolen base record with 118 in 1974. Name him.

Called 'Buck', this Negro Leagues first baseman was often compared to Lou Gehrig and played alongside Josh Gibson on the Homestead Grays. Name him.

He won the 1992 NL MVP after hitting .311 with 35 home runs and 112 RBIs for the Pittsburgh Pirates. Who was he?

This Dodgers pitcher threw a no-hitter on June 18, 2014, striking out 15 batters against the Rockies. Name him.

This closer recorded 478 career saves and was the first reliever to win the Cy Young Award in the AL, doing so in 1992. Name him.
Called 'Wizard', this Padres and Cardinals shortstop won 13 consecutive Gold Glove Awards. Name him.

This manager led the Dodgers from 1976 to 1996, winning two World Series titles and four NL pennants. Name him.
I pitched a no-hitter at Yankee Stadium on July 4, 1983, and finished my career with 324 wins. Who am I?
This outfielder won the 1979 NL MVP and World Series MVP for the Pittsburgh Pirates, earning the nickname 'Pops'. Name him.
He won the 2006 AL MVP after hitting .347 with 35 home runs for the Minnesota Twins. Who was he?
This reliever was known for his signature handlebar mustache and won the 1981 AL Cy Young and MVP Awards. Name him.

He holds the record for most consecutive innings pitched without allowing an earned run during a World Series career, with 33.2 scoreless innings. Who was he?

I won back-to-back NL MVP Awards in 1954 and 1955 while catching for the Brooklyn Dodgers. Who am I?

Called 'Goose', this intimidating reliever recorded 310 career saves and was inducted into the Hall of Fame in 2008. Name him.
This Twins catcher won three AL batting titles and the 2009 AL MVP Award. Name him.
I won 373 career games, tied for third on the all-time wins list, and threw two no-hitters for the Phillies. Who am I?
